selectionRule.summary: MVP starts with randomized city-stage selection. Formal release keeps all archetypes and expands them into unlockable or travelable regions.
selectionRule.aiGuidance: The AI may generate the final city name, district names, family context, and local rumors from the selected archetype. The archetype defines constraints, not a fixed map.

cityNamingRule.summary: MVP uses both benchmark cities and a reusable name pool. Benchmark cities provide stable testable tone; name pools provide run-to-run variation.
cityNamingRule.playerInput: The player cannot directly customize the city name during normal MVP character creation unless a later custom-world mode enables it.
cityNamingRule.playerFacingResult: The player only sees the final generated birth city, surface social context, and starting background. The UI does not expose whether the city came from a benchmark city or a name pool.
aiGuidance: If a benchmark city is selected, preserve its core premise, public level, and local truth themes.
If a name-pool city is selected, generate districts, local institutions, rumors, and family context from the selected archetype.
Do not show benchmark city selection as a player choice in MVP.
Never let the city name force the player character into an investigator route.
The same city can produce different life paths depending on identity, attributes, talents, family background, luck, exposure, and player choices.

benchmarkCities.name: Haijing
benchmarkCities.label: Sea-Mirror Coastal Metropolis
benchmarkCities.surfaceIdentity: A prosperous coastal metropolis known for universities, finance, ports, media companies, museums, and dense online rumor culture.

benchmarkCities.name: Mowen
benchmarkCities.label: Inkmark University City
benchmarkCities.surfaceIdentity: A modern university city built around campuses, libraries, teaching hospitals, laboratories, student forums, bookstores, and research institutes.
hiddenTruthThemes: library rooms that do not exist

benchmarkCities.name: Qingshi
benchmarkCities.label: Bluestone Old City
benchmarkCities.surfaceIdentity: An old city surrounded by quiet towns, ancestral halls, folk temples, old schools, clinics, mines, rivers, and tight family networks.

archetypes.label: Fictional Coastal Metropolis
archetypes.surface: A large coastal city with universities, hospitals, ports, finance, media, old districts, nightlife, and internet rumor culture.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Sea whispers, port disappearances, corporate laboratories, museum relics, dream infections, and sealed waterfront districts.

archetypes.label: Old City And Surrounding Towns
archetypes.surface: An old city surrounded by small towns, ancestral halls, old houses, local temples, mines, forests, rivers, and family networks.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Family curses, folk taboos, buried ruins, sealed villages, abnormal weddings and funerals, old bloodline contracts, and local silence.

archetypes.label: Modern University City
archetypes.surface: A planned education district with campuses, laboratories, libraries, hospitals, student apartments, bookstores, clubs, forums, and youth culture.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Forbidden archives, professor disappearances, dream experiments, secret societies, abnormal research projects, and student cult cells.

archetypes.label: Corporate Inland City
archetypes.surface: A fast-growing inland city shaped by corporations, industrial parks, private hospitals, residential compounds, surveillance systems, and class division.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Abnormal biotech, occult capital, controlled disappearances, company clinics, sealed labs, and executives bargaining with non-human powers.

archetypes.label: Border Industrial City
archetypes.surface: A harsh border or frontier industrial city with factories, freight stations, smuggling routes, cheap housing, abandoned zones, and transient populations.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Illegal rituals, black-market relics, contaminated workers, missing migrants, underground clinics, and forgotten war or disaster sites.

archetypes.label: Quiet Suburban Region
archetypes.surface: A quiet suburban region of schools, malls, clinics, parks, commuter rail, gated communities, family life, and mild local gossip.
archetypes.hiddenLayer: Dream contagion, neighbor disappearances, basement shrines, family replacements, children's imaginary friends, and normalcy slowly rotting from within.

mvpRules: MVP uses random city archetype selection instead of one fixed default city.
MVP uses benchmark cities plus a name pool: benchmark cities stabilize tone, while name-pool cities preserve replay variety.
Benchmark cities are hidden generation anchors in MVP. Players do not directly see or choose Haijing, Mowen, or Qingshi as presets.
The selected archetype should influence family background, local NPC identity anchors, event seeds, public explanation style, and available organizations.
A player character may move cities later if life events, family background, career, organizations, exile, pursuit, or supernatural pressure justify it.
City archetypes are constraints for AI generation, not fixed maps or forced plots.
formalRules: Formal release should keep all archetypes and expand them into richer regions.
Formal release may support travel, migration, unlockable cities, cross-city organizations, and player-customized world regions.
